title: Apparatus for backfilling a trench
abstract: Apparatus for backfilling a trench and, more particularly, to apparatus for laying a pipeline bed layer in a trench and for laying a pipeline bedding layer once a pipeline is laid in the trench. The apparatus comprises a multi-deck screen vibrating unit (4). The upper deck screen (4a) comprises finger tines (31) or a grizzly arrangement (41).
claims:
Claims Claims: Self-propelled apparatus (1) for backfilling a trench with filler material (9) while moving along a to be filled trench, the apparatus (1) comprising a feed hopper (2), a multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4) comprising at least two screens (4a, 4b), vibrating means (V) for vibrating the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4), a first means of transport (3) for feeding material from the feed hopper (2) to the multi- deck vibrating screen unit (4) and a further means of transport (5) for bringing the filler material (9) exiting the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4) to the to be filled trench, wherein the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4) comprises an upper deck (4a) comprising finger tines (31) and/or a grizzly screen (41), preferably finger tines (31), and at least one lower deck screen (4b) below the upper deck (4a) and a bottom side (6) below the lower deck screen (4b), the bottom side (6) forming a slide for filler material (9) passing the lower deck screen (4b), wherein the multi- deck vibrating screen unit (4) is tilted parallel to the direction of movement of the self-propelled apparatus (1), wherein the further means of transport (5) are located at the end of the bottom side (6) of the multi- vibrating screen unit (4) for collecting filler material (9) sliding off the bottom side (6) and the upper deck screen (4a) and the at least one lower deck screen (4b) each extending beyond the further means of transport (5) and wherein the further means of transport (5) extend on either side of the self-propelled apparatus (1). Self-propelled apparatus (1) as claimed in claim 1 wherein the upper deck screen (4a) is a cascaded deck with finger bars (31). Self-propelled apparatus as claimed in claim 1 wherein the upper deck screen (4a) comprises at an end facing the first means of transport (3) a grizzly structure and at an end remote from the first means of transport (3) finger tines (31). Self-propelled apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the lower deck screen (4b) below the upper deck screen (4a) comprises a series of cascade bars, smaller in size and at a smaller distance to each other than the finger tines. Self-propelled apparatus as claimed in claim 1, 2 or 3 wherein the lower deck screen (4b) below the upper deck screen (4a) comprises a self cleaning bottom deck screen Mesh. 

 Self-propelled apparatus as claimed in any of the preceding claims wherein the apparatus comprises a tilting mechanism (H) for adjusting the angle of tilt of the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4). Self-propelled apparatus as claimed in any of the preceding claims wherein the upper or lower deck comprises finger tines (31), and means (P, F) for adjusting the angle of the finger tines. Self-propelled apparatus as claimed in any of the preceding claims wherein the vibrating means are positioned in between the upper and lower deck. Method for backfilling a trench wherein a self-propelled apparatus (1) for backfilling a trench as claimed in any of the preceding claims moves along a to be filled trench wherein raw material is fed into the feed hopper (2) and is transported by the transport means (3) to the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4), and while moving, the raw material is segregated in the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4) into waste material (8) and filler material (9), the filler material (9) slides of the bottom (6) of the multi-deck vibrating screen unit (4) onto the further transport means (5) and the filler material (9) is transported sideways to fill a trench by the further transport means (5) wherein the waste material (8) exits the vibrating unit (4) at an end of the vibrating unit (4) beyond the further transport means (5). Method as claimed in claim 9 wherein in the trench a pipeline (10) is positioned and the filler material (9) is deposited on the pipeline (10).
